title: buxus foliosa
intro: Buxus foliosa, also known as boxwood, is a small, evergreen shrub native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly in Latin American cultures, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Buxus foliosa include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate skin conditions, and treat respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the leaves and roots of the plant are typically used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
